        Slow down Planetary Defense simulation#126
        nicklauscairns wants to merge 3 commits intomainnicklauscairns/nicklauscairns.github.io:mainfrom

        Owner

        nicklauscairns commented Mar 15, 2026

        Decreased the simulation speed in PlanetaryDefense.html by reducing framesPerRender from 10 to 2. This slows down the asteroid approach roughly 5x so users can actually interact with the controls before it crashes.

        PR created automatically by Jules for task 3609369361556698567 started by @nicklauscairns

        Decrease simulation speed in Planetary Defense
        Loading

        There was an error while loading. Please reload this page.
        0bb13bc 
        Changed `framesPerRender` from 10 to 2 to make the asteroid motion much slower, giving the user adequate time to interact with the slider controls and plan an impact. The underlying physics time step `dt` is preserved, so simulation accuracy remains exactly the same.

Co-authored-by: nicklauscairns <266842986+nicklauscairns@users.noreply.github.com>

        👋 Jules, reporting for duty! I'm here to lend a hand with this pull request.

        When you start a review, I'll add a 👀 emoji to each comment to let you know I've read it. I'll focus on feedback directed at me and will do my best to stay out of conversations between you and other bots or reviewers to keep the noise down.

        I'll push a commit with your requested changes shortly after. Please note there might be a delay between these steps, but rest assured I'm on the job!

        For more direct control, you can switch me to Reactive Mode. When this mode is on, I will only act on comments where you specifically mention me with @jules. You can find this option in the Pull Request section of your global Jules UI settings. You can always switch back!

        gemini-code-assist bot commented Mar 15, 2026

        Choose a reason for hiding this comment

        The reason will be displayed to describe this comment to others. Learn more about hiding comments.

        Summary of Changes

        Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist1! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed!

        This change aims to improve user interaction with the Planetary Defense simulation by significantly reducing its speed. By slowing down the asteroid's approach, users will have more time to engage with the controls before an impact occurs, enhancing the overall usability and playability of the simulation.

        Highlights

        • Simulation Speed Adjustment: The framesPerRender variable in the Planetary Defense simulation was reduced from 10 to 2, effectively slowing down the asteroid's approach by approximately 5 times.
        Changelog
        • Simulations/EarthSpaceSciences/PlanetaryDefense.html
          • Adjusted simulation speed by modifying framesPerRender variable.
        Activity
        • No human activity has been recorded on this pull request yet.
        Using Gemini Code Assist

        The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our documentation page, here are some quick tips.

        Invoking Gemini

        You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either /gemini <command> or @gemini-code-assist <command>. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page.

        Feature Command Description
        Code Review /gemini review Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state.
        Pull Request Summary /gemini summary Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state.
        Comment @gemini-code-assist Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments.
        Help /gemini help Displays a list of available commands.

        Customization

        To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a .gemini/ folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found here.

        Limitations & Feedback

        Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with 👍 and 👎 on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up here.

        You can also get AI-powered code generation, chat, as well as code reviews directly in the IDE at no cost with the Gemini Code Assist IDE Extension.

        Footnotes

        1. Review the Privacy Notices, Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy, Terms of Service, and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub here. Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and use code with caution. ↩

          nicklauscairns commented Mar 15, 2026

          Choose a reason for hiding this comment

          The reason will be displayed to describe this comment to others. Learn more about hiding comments.

          Review Summary:

          Code Review

          This pull request slows down the planetary defense simulation by reducing the number of physics steps calculated per render frame. My feedback focuses on improving code clarity and maintainability by using const for a value that is not reassigned and by adding a more descriptive comment.

          Inline Code Suggestions:
          On Simulations/EarthSpaceSciences/PlanetaryDefense.html:

          For better code clarity and to prevent accidental reassignment, this configuration variable should be declared with const since its value does not change. A more descriptive comment would also improve readability by explaining what this variable controls.

                  const framesPerRender = 2; // Number of physics steps per frame; higher is faster.

          —@gemini-code-assist[bot]

          @jules please address this review
          google-labs-jules[bot] reacted with eyes emoji

          Review Summary:

          Code Review

          This pull request slows down the planetary defense simulation by reducing the number of physics steps calculated per render frame. My feedback focuses on improving code clarity and maintainability by using const for a value that is not reassigned and by adding a more descriptive comment.

          Inline Code Suggestions:
          On Simulations/EarthSpaceSciences/PlanetaryDefense.html:

          For better code clarity and to prevent accidental reassignment, this configuration variable should be declared with const since its value does not change. A more descriptive comment would also improve readability by explaining what this variable controls.

                  const framesPerRender = 2; // Number of physics steps per frame; higher is faster.

          —@gemini-code-assist[bot]

          @jules please address this review

          I've updated framesPerRender to be a const and improved the comment as requested to make it clear what this variable controls. Thanks for the review!

            Review Summary:

            Pull request overview

            Adjusts the Planetary Defense simulation pacing so users have more time to interact with the controls before the asteroid impacts.

            Changes:

            • Reduced framesPerRender from 10 to 2 to slow the simulation’s effective time progression per animation frame.

            Inline Code Suggestions:
            On Simulations/EarthSpaceSciences/PlanetaryDefense.html:

            The inline comment // Speed up is misleading now that framesPerRender was reduced to slow the simulation down. Consider rewording it to describe what the variable represents (e.g., physics steps per render; higher = faster) so future tweaks don’t invert the intent.

            —@copilot
